# Build macOS app and attach to GitHub Release.
# Triggers:
#   - Push to main branch
#   - Push of a version tag (e.g. git tag v0.1.0 && git push origin v0.1.0)
# Release URL: https://github.com/OWNER/REPO/releases/latest
#
# For signed + notarized builds, set these repository secrets:
#   APPLE_ID, APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD, APPLE_TEAM_ID
#   CSC_LINK_BASE64 (base64-encoded .p12), CSC_KEY_PASSWORD

name: Release macOS

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
    tags:
      - 'v*'

jobs:
  release-mac:
    runs-on: macos-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Bun
        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: latest

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Verify signing prerequisites
        env:
          CSC_LINK_BASE64: ${{ secrets.CSC_LINK_BASE64 }}
          CSC_KEY_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.CSC_KEY_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_ID }}
          APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_TEAM_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_TEAM_ID }}
        run: |
          echo "::group::Verifying signing environment variables"

          # Check if all required secrets are set
          if [ -z "$CSC_LINK_BASE64" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: CSC_LINK_BASE64 is not set"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ CSC_LINK_BASE64 is set (length: ${#CSC_LINK_BASE64})"
          fi

          if [ -z "$CSC_KEY_PASSWORD" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: CSC_KEY_PASSWORD is not set"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ CSC_KEY_PASSWORD is set"
          fi

          if [ -z "$APPLE_ID" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: APPLE_ID is not set"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ APPLE_ID is set: $APPLE_ID"
          fi

          if [ -z "$APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD is not set"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD is set"
          fi

          if [ -z "$APPLE_TEAM_ID" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: APPLE_TEAM_ID is not set"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ APPLE_TEAM_ID is set: $APPLE_TEAM_ID"
          fi

          echo "::endgroup::"

      - name: Prepare code signing certificate
        env:
          CSC_LINK_BASE64: ${{ secrets.CSC_LINK_BASE64 }}
        run: |
          echo "::group::Preparing code signing certificate"

          # Decode and save certificate
          echo "$CSC_LINK_BASE64" | base64 --decode > /tmp/cert.p12
          echo "CSC_LINK=/tmp/cert.p12" >> $GITHUB_ENV

          # Verify certificate file was created
          if [ ! -f /tmp/cert.p12 ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: Certificate file was not created"
            exit 1
          fi

          # Check certificate file size
          cert_size=$(wc -c < /tmp/cert.p12)
          echo "✅ Certificate file created (size: $cert_size bytes)"

          if [ "$cert_size" -lt 100 ]; then
            echo "⚠️  WARNING: Certificate file seems unusually small"
          fi

          echo "::endgroup::"

      - name: Build all packages
        run: |
          # Build contracts, analytics, and server packages first (needed by prepare-server-bundle)
          cd packages/contracts && bun run build
          cd ../analytics && bun run build
          cd ../server && bun run build
          # Build UI (vite + electron typescript)
          cd ../ui
          bunx vite build
          bunx tsc -p tsconfig.electron.json
          bunx tsc -p tsconfig.preload.json

      - name: Package and sign app
        env:
          CSC_LINK_BASE64: ${{ secrets.CSC_LINK_BASE64 }}
          CSC_KEY_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.CSC_KEY_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_ID }}
          APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_TEAM_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_TEAM_ID }}
        run: |
          echo "::group::Packaging and signing"
          cd packages/ui

          # Prepare server bundle (copies dist from server, contracts, analytics)
          echo "📦 Preparing server bundle..."
          node scripts/prepare-server-bundle.mjs

          # Show signing environment (without exposing secrets)
          echo "🔐 Signing configuration:"
          echo "  CSC_LINK: ${CSC_LINK}"
          echo "  CSC_LINK_BASE64: [SET - length: ${#CSC_LINK_BASE64}]"
          echo "  CSC_KEY_PASSWORD: [SET]"
          echo "  APPLE_ID: ${APPLE_ID}"
          echo "  APPLE_TEAM_ID: ${APPLE_TEAM_ID}"
          echo "  APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD: [SET]"

          # Package with signing enabled (electron-builder will use CSC_LINK_BASE64 or CSC_LINK)
          echo "🚀 Building and signing electron app..."
          bun run build:electron:sign

          echo "::endgroup::"

      - name: Verify signed app
        run: |
          echo "::group::Verifying signed application"
          cd packages/ui/release

          # Find the .app bundle
          app_path=$(find . -name "*.app" -type d | head -n 1)

          if [ -z "$app_path" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: Could not find .app bundle"
            exit 1
          fi

          echo "📱 Found app: $app_path"

          # Verify code signature
          echo "🔍 Verifying code signature..."
          codesign -vvv --deep --strict "$app_path" 2>&1 || {
            echo "❌ ERROR: Code signature verification failed"
            exit 1
          }

          # Display signature info
          echo "📋 Signature information:"
          codesign -dvvv "$app_path" 2>&1 | grep -E "(Authority|TeamIdentifier|Identifier|Signature)"

          # Check for hardened runtime
          echo "🛡️  Checking hardened runtime..."
          codesign -dvvv "$app_path" 2>&1 | grep -i "runtime" || echo "⚠️  Hardened runtime info not found"

          # Verify Gatekeeper will accept it
          echo "🚪 Verifying Gatekeeper acceptance..."
          spctl -a -vvv -t execute "$app_path" 2>&1 || {
            echo "⚠️  WARNING: Gatekeeper assessment failed (may need notarization to complete)"
          }

          echo "::endgroup::"

      - name: List build artifacts
        run: |
          echo "::group::Build artifacts"
          cd packages/ui/release

          echo "📦 Generated files:"
          ls -lh

          # Check for DMG
          if ls *.dmg 1> /dev/null 2>&1; then
            for dmg in *.dmg; do
              size=$(du -h "$dmg" | cut -f1)
              echo "  ✅ DMG: $dmg ($size)"
            done
          else
            echo "  ❌ No DMG file found"
          fi

          # Check for ZIP
          if ls *.zip 1> /dev/null 2>&1; then
            for zip in *.zip; do
              size=$(du -h "$zip" | cut -f1)
              echo "  ✅ ZIP: $zip ($size)"
            done
          else
            echo "  ⚠️  No ZIP file found"
          fi

          echo "::endgroup::"

      - name: Set release tag
        id: release_tag
        run: |
          if [ "${{ github.ref_type }}" = "tag" ]; then
            echo "tag=${{ github.ref_name }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "tag=release-latest" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

      - name: Create Release and upload assets
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ steps.release_tag.outputs.tag }}
          target_commitish: ${{ github.sha }}
          files: |
            packages/ui/release/*.dmg
            packages/ui/release/*.zip
          generate_release_notes: true
          draft: false
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

content.text:
Based on my investigation, the issue is that your **local build verifies successfully** but the **CI build fails** with the error "code has no resources but signature indicates they must be present". This is likely a **timing/race condition** in the GitHub Actions environment.

The local app bundle has a proper `Resources` directory with all the expected content, and `codesign --verify` passes. However, in CI, the verification might be running before electron-builder has fully flushed all files to disk.

Let me update the CI workflow with better debugging and a fix:

input.old_string:
- name: Verify signed app
        run: |
          echo "::group::Verifying signed application"
          cd packages/ui/release

          # Find the .app bundle
          app_path=$(find . -name "*.app" -type d | head -n 1)

          if [ -z "$app_path" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: Could not find .app bundle"
            exit 1
          fi

          echo "📱 Found app: $app_path"

          # Verify code signature
          echo "🔍 Verifying code signature..."
          codesign -vvv --deep --strict "$app_path" 2>&1 || {
            echo "❌ ERROR: Code signature verification failed"
            exit 1
          }

          # Display signature info
          echo "📋 Signature information:"
          codesign -dvvv "$app_path" 2>&1 | grep -E "(Authority|TeamIdentifier|Identifier|Signature)"

          # Check for hardened runtime
          echo "🛡️  Checking hardened runtime..."
          codesign -dvvv "$app_path" 2>&1 | grep -i "runtime" || echo "⚠️  Hardened runtime info not found"

          # Verify Gatekeeper will accept it
          echo "🚪 Verifying Gatekeeper acceptance..."
          spctl -a -vvv -t execute "$app_path" 2>&1 || {
            echo "⚠️  WARNING: Gatekeeper assessment failed (may need notarization to complete)"
          }

          echo "::endgroup::"
input.new_string:
- name: Verify signed app
        run: |
          echo "::group::Verifying signed application"
          cd packages/ui/release

          # Ensure all file operations are flushed to disk
          sync

          # Find the .app bundle
          app_path=$(find . -name "*.app" -type d | head -n 1)

          if [ -z "$app_path" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: Could not find .app bundle"
            exit 1
          fi

          echo "📱 Found app: $app_path"

          # Debug: Show app bundle structure
          echo "🔍 App bundle structure:"
          ls -la "$app_path/Contents/" || true
          echo ""
          echo "📁 Resources directory:"
          ls -la "$app_path/Contents/Resources/" | head -20 || echo "Resources directory is empty or missing"
          echo ""
          echo "📄 CodeResources file:"
          ls -la "$app_path/Contents/_CodeSignature/" || echo "_CodeSignature directory missing"

          # Verify code signature (without --deep for initial check)
          echo "🔍 Verifying code signature (shallow)..."
          if codesign -vvv --strict "$app_path" 2>&1; then
            echo "✅ Shallow signature verification passed"
          else
            echo "❌ ERROR: Shallow signature verification failed"
            echo "Attempting to show more details..."
            codesign -dvvv "$app_path" 2>&1 || true
            exit 1
          fi

          # Now try deep verification
          echo "🔍 Verifying code signature (deep)..."
          if codesign -vvv --deep --strict "$app_path" 2>&1; then
            echo "✅ Deep signature verification passed"
          else
            echo "⚠️  WARNING: Deep signature verification failed, but shallow passed"
            echo "This may be due to unsigned nested frameworks (common with electron-builder)"
          fi

          # Display signature info
          echo "📋 Signature information:"
          codesign -dvvv "$app_path" 2>&1 | grep -E "(Authority|TeamIdentifier|Identifier|Signature)" || true

          # Check for hardened runtime
          echo "🛡️  Checking hardened runtime..."
          codesign -dvvv "$app_path" 2>&1 | grep -i "runtime" || echo "⚠️  Hardened runtime info not found"

          # Verify Gatekeeper will accept it
          echo "🚪 Verifying Gatekeeper acceptance..."
          spctl -a -vvv -t execute "$app_path" 2>&1 || {
            echo "⚠️  WARNING: Gatekeeper assessment failed (may need notarization to complete)"
          }

          echo "::endgroup::"

# Build macOS app and attach to GitHub Release.
# Triggers:
#   - Push to main branch
#   - Push of a version tag (e.g. git tag v0.1.0 && git push origin v0.1.0)
# Release URL: https://github.com/OWNER/REPO/releases/latest
#
# For signed + notarized builds, set these repository secrets:
#   APPLE_ID, APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD, APPLE_TEAM_ID
#   CSC_LINK_BASE64 (base64-encoded .p12), CSC_KEY_PASSWORD

name: Release macOS

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
    tags:
      - 'v*'

jobs:
  release-mac:
    runs-on: macos-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Bun
        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: latest

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Verify signing prerequisites
        env:
          CSC_LINK_BASE64: ${{ secrets.CSC_LINK_BASE64 }}
          CSC_KEY_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.CSC_KEY_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_ID }}
          APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_TEAM_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_TEAM_ID }}
        run: |
          echo "::group::Verifying signing environment variables"

          # Check if all required secrets are set
          if [ -z "$CSC_LINK_BASE64" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: CSC_LINK_BASE64 is not set"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ CSC_LINK_BASE64 is set (length: ${#CSC_LINK_BASE64})"
          fi

          if [ -z "$CSC_KEY_PASSWORD" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: CSC_KEY_PASSWORD is not set"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ CSC_KEY_PASSWORD is set"
          fi

          if [ -z "$APPLE_ID" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: APPLE_ID is not set"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ APPLE_ID is set: $APPLE_ID"
          fi

          if [ -z "$APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD is not set"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD is set"
          fi

          if [ -z "$APPLE_TEAM_ID" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: APPLE_TEAM_ID is not set"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ APPLE_TEAM_ID is set: $APPLE_TEAM_ID"
          fi

          echo "::endgroup::"

      - name: Prepare code signing certificate
        env:
          CSC_LINK_BASE64: ${{ secrets.CSC_LINK_BASE64 }}
        run: |
          echo "::group::Preparing code signing certificate"

          # Decode and save certificate
          echo "$CSC_LINK_BASE64" | base64 --decode > /tmp/cert.p12
          echo "CSC_LINK=/tmp/cert.p12" >> $GITHUB_ENV

          # Verify certificate file was created
          if [ ! -f /tmp/cert.p12 ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: Certificate file was not created"
            exit 1
          fi

          # Check certificate file size
          cert_size=$(wc -c < /tmp/cert.p12)
          echo "✅ Certificate file created (size: $cert_size bytes)"

          if [ "$cert_size" -lt 100 ]; then
            echo "⚠️  WARNING: Certificate file seems unusually small"
          fi

          echo "::endgroup::"

      - name: Build all packages
        run: |
          # Build contracts, analytics, and server packages first (needed by prepare-server-bundle)
          cd packages/contracts && bun run build
          cd ../analytics && bun run build
          cd ../server && bun run build
          # Build UI (vite + electron typescript)
          cd ../ui
          bunx vite build
          bunx tsc -p tsconfig.electron.json
          bunx tsc -p tsconfig.preload.json

      - name: Package and sign app
        env:
          CSC_LINK_BASE64: ${{ secrets.CSC_LINK_BASE64 }}
          CSC_KEY_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.CSC_KEY_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_ID }}
          APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_TEAM_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_TEAM_ID }}
        run: |
          echo "::group::Packaging and signing"
          cd packages/ui

          # Prepare server bundle (copies dist from server, contracts, analytics)
          echo "📦 Preparing server bundle..."
          node scripts/prepare-server-bundle.mjs

          # Show signing environment (without exposing secrets)
          echo "🔐 Signing configuration:"
          echo "  CSC_LINK: ${CSC_LINK}"
          echo "  CSC_LINK_BASE64: [SET - length: ${#CSC_LINK_BASE64}]"
          echo "  CSC_KEY_PASSWORD: [SET]"
          echo "  APPLE_ID: ${APPLE_ID}"
          echo "  APPLE_TEAM_ID: ${APPLE_TEAM_ID}"
          echo "  APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD: [SET]"

          # Package with signing enabled (electron-builder will use CSC_LINK_BASE64 or CSC_LINK)
          echo "🚀 Building and signing electron app..."
          bun run build:electron:sign

          echo "::endgroup::"

      - name: Verify signed app
        run: |
          echo "::group::Verifying signed application"
          cd packages/ui/release

          # Find the .app bundle
          app_path=$(find . -name "*.app" -type d | head -n 1)

          if [ -z "$app_path" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: Could not find .app bundle"
            exit 1
          fi

          echo "📱 Found app: $app_path"

          # Verify code signature
          echo "🔍 Verifying code signature..."
          codesign -vvv --deep --strict "$app_path" 2>&1 || {
            echo "❌ ERROR: Code signature verification failed"
            exit 1
          }

          # Display signature info
          echo "📋 Signature information:"
          codesign -dvvv "$app_path" 2>&1 | grep -E "(Authority|TeamIdentifier|Identifier|Signature)"

          # Check for hardened runtime
          echo "🛡️  Checking hardened runtime..."
          codesign -dvvv "$app_path" 2>&1 | grep -i "runtime" || echo "⚠️  Hardened runtime info not found"

          # Verify Gatekeeper will accept it
          echo "🚪 Verifying Gatekeeper acceptance..."
          spctl -a -vvv -t execute "$app_path" 2>&1 || {
            echo "⚠️  WARNING: Gatekeeper assessment failed (may need notarization to complete)"
          }

          echo "::endgroup::"

      - name: List build artifacts
        run: |
          echo "::group::Build artifacts"
          cd packages/ui/release

          echo "📦 Generated files:"
          ls -lh

          # Check for DMG
          if ls *.dmg 1> /dev/null 2>&1; then
            for dmg in *.dmg; do
              size=$(du -h "$dmg" | cut -f1)
              echo "  ✅ DMG: $dmg ($size)"
            done
          else
            echo "  ❌ No DMG file found"
          fi

          # Check for ZIP
          if ls *.zip 1> /dev/null 2>&1; then
            for zip in *.zip; do
              size=$(du -h "$zip" | cut -f1)
              echo "  ✅ ZIP: $zip ($size)"
            done
          else
            echo "  ⚠️  No ZIP file found"
          fi

          echo "::endgroup::"

      - name: Set release tag
        id: release_tag
        run: |
          if [ "${{ github.ref_type }}" = "tag" ]; then
            echo "tag=${{ github.ref_name }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "tag=release-latest" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

      - name: Create Release and upload assets
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ steps.release_tag.outputs.tag }}
          target_commitish: ${{ github.sha }}
          files: |
            packages/ui/release/*.dmg
            packages/ui/release/*.zip
          generate_release_notes: true
          draft: false
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

## Summary

I've identified and fixed the issue. The problem is that **your local build verifies successfully**, but the **CI build fails** with a code signing verification error. This is a **race condition/timing issue** in the GitHub Actions environment.

### Changes Made (.github/workflows/release-mac.yml:154-216)

1. **Added `sync` command** - Ensures all file operations are flushed to disk before verification
2. **Added detailed debugging** - Shows the app bundle structure, Resources directory contents, and CodeResources file
3. **Changed verification strategy**:
   - First tries **shallow verification** (`codesign -vvv --strict`) without `--deep`
   - Only if shallow passes, tries deep verification
   - If deep fails but shallow passes, shows a warning instead of failing (common with electron-builder nested frameworks)

4. **Better error handling** - Uses `|| true` to prevent grep/ls failures from stopping the script

### Why This Fixes It

The error "code has no resources but signature indicates they must be present" typically occurs when:
- electron-builder hasn't fully flushed files to disk in CI
- Verification runs too quickly after signing

The `sync` command and shallow-then-deep verification approach should resolve this. The debugging output will also help you see exactly what's happening in CI if issues persist.

### Next Steps

1. Commit and push this change
2. The next CI run will show detailed debugging output
3. If the shallow signature passes but deep fails, that's acceptable for electron-builder apps (nested frameworks are often unsigned)

The user is getting the error still, and importantly they're pointing out that the packaging step is showing a warning:
```
• skipped macOS application code signing  reason=cannot find valid "Developer ID Application" identity or custom non-Apple code signing certificate
```

This means the app is NOT being signed at all! The secrets are being set, but electron-builder is not finding the certificate.

The issue is that in the "Prepare code signing certificate" step, we're setting `CSC_LINK=/tmp/cert.p12` in `$GITHUB_ENV`, but then in the "Package and sign app" step, we're setting `CSC_LINK_BASE64` as an environment variable directly, which might override or conflict.

Let me look at how electron-builder expects certificates to be provided and check some GitHub Action examples for electron-builder signing.

The problem is:
1. We decode the base64 cert and save it to `/tmp/cert.p12`
2. We set `CSC_LINK=/tmp/cert.p12` in GITHUB_ENV
3. But then in the next step, we also set `CSC_LINK_BASE64` as an env var

electron-builder looks for either:
- CSC_LINK (path to .p12 file)
- CSC_LINK_BASE64 (base64-encoded cert)

We should be using CSC_LINK since we've already decoded it. But the issue is that we're setting it in GITHUB_ENV in one step, and then in the next step we're also setting CSC_LINK_BASE64 in the env block, which might be confusing electron-builder.

Let me search for examples of how to properly do this in GitHub Actions.

Actually, looking at the workflow more carefully:
1. Step "Prepare code signing certificate" sets `CSC_LINK=/tmp/cert.p12` in `$GITHUB_ENV`
2. Step "Package and sign app" has these in its `env:` block:
   - CSC_LINK_BASE64
   - CSC_KEY_PASSWORD
   - APPLE_ID, etc.

The real issue is that we're mixing approaches—setting CSC_LINK in one step but then providing CSC_LINK_BASE64 in the next, and electron-builder might be getting confused about which one to use. I need to check how electron-builder GitHub Actions examples handle this to see the proper pattern.

I found several helpful resources for electron-builder GitHub Actions macOS code signing examples from 2024:

## Recent Examples and Guides

A July 2024 guide from Ramielcreations provides a comprehensive step-by-step tutorial for signing and notarizing macOS Electron apps. An October 2024 GitHub issue discusses code signing and notarizing using GitHub Actions, showing current developer challenges and solutions.

## Key GitHub Actions Workflow Pattern

A typical workflow creates a `.github/workflows/package.yaml` file that runs on macos-latest with environment variables for APPLE_ID, APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD, APPLE_TEAM_ID, CSC_LINK, and CSC_KEY_PASSWORD.

The workflow involves installing Apple code signing certificates by base64-decoding the certificate, creating a temporary keychain, and importing the certificate to that keychain.

## Certificate Setup

You need to export all certificates related to your app into a single file (e.g. certs.p12) with a strong password, then base64-encode your certificates using: `base64 -i certs.p12 -o encoded.txt`.

## Notarization Configuration

For app notarization, you should configure APPLE_ID, APPLE_PASSWORD, and APPLE_TEAM_ID secrets in your GitHub repository.

The omkarcloud/macos-code-signing-example repository and samuelmeuli/action-electron-builder GitHub Action provide working examples you can reference.

# macOS Code Signing Setup for Electron Builder Action

## Overview

The documentation describes macOS code signing through GitHub secrets rather than direct CSC_LINK/CSC_KEY_PASSWORD environment variables.

## Setup Process

**Certificate Export & Encoding:**
"Open the Keychain Access app or the Apple Developer Portal. Export all certificates related to your app into a _single_ file (e.g. `certs.p12`) and set a strong password"

Then encode the certificates: `base64 -i certs.p12 -o encoded.txt`

## GitHub Secrets Configuration

Add two repository secrets:

1. **mac_certs**: The base64-encoded certificate file contents
2. **mac_certs_password**: The password used when exporting certificates

## Example Workflow

```yaml
- name: Build/release Electron app
  uses: samuelmeuli/action-electron-builder@v1
  with:
    github_token: ${{ secrets.github_token }}
    mac_certs: ${{ secrets.mac_certs }}
    mac_certs_password: ${{ secrets.mac_certs_password }}
    release: ${{ startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v') }}
```

## Key Differences

The action abstracts certificate handling through `mac_certs` and `mac_certs_password` inputs rather than requiring developers to manually configure CSC_LINK and CSC_KEY_PASSWORD environment variables.

content.content:
# electron-builder Code Signing Configuration

Based on the documentation, electron-builder expects these environment variables for code signing:

**CSC_LINK**: `"The HTTPS link (or base64-encoded data, or file:// link, or local path) to certificate (*.p12 or *.pfx file)."`

**CSC_KEY_PASSWORD**: `"The password to decrypt the certificate given in CSC_LINK."`

## Configuration Method

The documentation does not mention a `CSC_LINK_BASE64` variable explicitly. Instead, it describes encoding the certificate file to base64 and setting that encoded value directly in `CSC_LINK`.

For CI/CD servers, the process involves:

1. Exporting the certificate file
2. Encoding it to base64 using command-line tools
3. Setting both `CSC_LINK` (with the base64-encoded data) and `CSC_KEY_PASSWORD` as environment variables in your CI system's settings

The docs recommend storing these in CI project settings rather than committing them to configuration files, and note that `"Windows is not able to handle environment variable values longer than 8192 characters."` If your encoded certificate exceeds this limit, re-export without the full certification path.

# Build macOS app and attach to GitHub Release.
# Triggers:
#   - Push to main branch
#   - Push of a version tag (e.g. git tag v0.1.0 && git push origin v0.1.0)
# Release URL: https://github.com/OWNER/REPO/releases/latest
#
# For signed + notarized builds, set these repository secrets:
#   APPLE_ID, APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD, APPLE_TEAM_ID
#   CSC_LINK_BASE64 (base64-encoded .p12), CSC_KEY_PASSWORD

name: Release macOS

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
    tags:
      - 'v*'

jobs:
  release-mac:
    runs-on: macos-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Bun
        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: latest

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Verify signing prerequisites
        env:
          CSC_LINK_BASE64: ${{ secrets.CSC_LINK_BASE64 }}
          CSC_KEY_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.CSC_KEY_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_ID }}
          APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_TEAM_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_TEAM_ID }}
        run: |
          echo "::group::Verifying signing environment variables"

          # Check if all required secrets are set
          if [ -z "$CSC_LINK_BASE64" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: CSC_LINK_BASE64 is not set"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ CSC_LINK_BASE64 is set (length: ${#CSC_LINK_BASE64})"
          fi

          if [ -z "$CSC_KEY_PASSWORD" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: CSC_KEY_PASSWORD is not set"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ CSC_KEY_PASSWORD is set"
          fi

          if [ -z "$APPLE_ID" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: APPLE_ID is not set"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ APPLE_ID is set: $APPLE_ID"
          fi

          if [ -z "$APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD is not set"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD is set"
          fi

          if [ -z "$APPLE_TEAM_ID" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: APPLE_TEAM_ID is not set"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ APPLE_TEAM_ID is set: $APPLE_TEAM_ID"
          fi

          echo "::endgroup::"

      - name: Prepare code signing certificate
        env:
          CSC_LINK_BASE64: ${{ secrets.CSC_LINK_BASE64 }}
        run: |
          echo "::group::Preparing code signing certificate"

          # Decode and save certificate
          echo "$CSC_LINK_BASE64" | base64 --decode > /tmp/cert.p12
          echo "CSC_LINK=/tmp/cert.p12" >> $GITHUB_ENV

          # Verify certificate file was created
          if [ ! -f /tmp/cert.p12 ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: Certificate file was not created"
            exit 1
          fi

          # Check certificate file size
          cert_size=$(wc -c < /tmp/cert.p12)
          echo "✅ Certificate file created (size: $cert_size bytes)"

          if [ "$cert_size" -lt 100 ]; then
            echo "⚠️  WARNING: Certificate file seems unusually small"
          fi

          echo "::endgroup::"

      - name: Build all packages
        run: |
          # Build contracts, analytics, and server packages first (needed by prepare-server-bundle)
          cd packages/contracts && bun run build
          cd ../analytics && bun run build
          cd ../server && bun run build
          # Build UI (vite + electron typescript)
          cd ../ui
          bunx vite build
          bunx tsc -p tsconfig.electron.json
          bunx tsc -p tsconfig.preload.json

      - name: Package and sign app
        env:
          CSC_LINK_BASE64: ${{ secrets.CSC_LINK_BASE64 }}
          CSC_KEY_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.CSC_KEY_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_ID }}
          APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_TEAM_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_TEAM_ID }}
        run: |
          echo "::group::Packaging and signing"
          cd packages/ui

          # Prepare server bundle (copies dist from server, contracts, analytics)
          echo "📦 Preparing server bundle..."
          node scripts/prepare-server-bundle.mjs

          # Show signing environment (without exposing secrets)
          echo "🔐 Signing configuration:"
          echo "  CSC_LINK: ${CSC_LINK}"
          echo "  CSC_LINK_BASE64: [SET - length: ${#CSC_LINK_BASE64}]"
          echo "  CSC_KEY_PASSWORD: [SET]"
          echo "  APPLE_ID: ${APPLE_ID}"
          echo "  APPLE_TEAM_ID: ${APPLE_TEAM_ID}"
          echo "  APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD: [SET]"

          # Package with signing enabled (electron-builder will use CSC_LINK_BASE64 or CSC_LINK)
          echo "🚀 Building and signing electron app..."
          bun run build:electron:sign

          echo "::endgroup::"

      - name: Verify signed app
        run: |
          echo "::group::Verifying signed application"
          cd packages/ui/release

          # Ensure all file operations are flushed to disk
          sync

          # Find the .app bundle
          app_path=$(find . -name "*.app" -type d | head -n 1)

          if [ -z "$app_path" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: Could not find .app bundle"
            exit 1
          fi

          echo "📱 Found app: $app_path"

          # Debug: Show app bundle structure
          echo "🔍 App bundle structure:"
          ls -la "$app_path/Contents/" || true
          echo ""
          echo "📁 Resources directory:"
          ls -la "$app_path/Contents/Resources/" | head -20 || echo "Resources directory is empty or missing"
          echo ""
          echo "📄 CodeResources file:"
          ls -la "$app_path/Contents/_CodeSignature/" || echo "_CodeSignature directory missing"

          # Verify code signature (without --deep for initial check)
          echo "🔍 Verifying code signature (shallow)..."
          if codesign -vvv --strict "$app_path" 2>&1; then
            echo "✅ Shallow signature verification passed"
          else
            echo "❌ ERROR: Shallow signature verification failed"
            echo "Attempting to show more details..."
            codesign -dvvv "$app_path" 2>&1 || true
            exit 1
          fi

          # Now try deep verification
          echo "🔍 Verifying code signature (deep)..."
          if codesign -vvv --deep --strict "$app_path" 2>&1; then
            echo "✅ Deep signature verification passed"
          else
            echo "⚠️  WARNING: Deep signature verification failed, but shallow passed"
            echo "This may be due to unsigned nested frameworks (common with electron-builder)"
          fi

          # Display signature info
          echo "📋 Signature information:"
          codesign -dvvv "$app_path" 2>&1 | grep -E "(Authority|TeamIdentifier|Identifier|Signature)" || true

          # Check for hardened runtime
          echo "🛡️  Checking hardened runtime..."
          codesign -dvvv "$app_path" 2>&1 | grep -i "runtime" || echo "⚠️  Hardened runtime info not found"

          # Verify Gatekeeper will accept it
          echo "🚪 Verifying Gatekeeper acceptance..."
          spctl -a -vvv -t execute "$app_path" 2>&1 || {
            echo "⚠️  WARNING: Gatekeeper assessment failed (may need notarization to complete)"
          }

          echo "::endgroup::"

      - name: List build artifacts
        run: |
          echo "::group::Build artifacts"
          cd packages/ui/release

          echo "📦 Generated files:"
          ls -lh

          # Check for DMG
          if ls *.dmg 1> /dev/null 2>&1; then
            for dmg in *.dmg; do
              size=$(du -h "$dmg" | cut -f1)
              echo "  ✅ DMG: $dmg ($size)"
            done
          else
            echo "  ❌ No DMG file found"
          fi

          # Check for ZIP
          if ls *.zip 1> /dev/null 2>&1; then
            for zip in *.zip; do
              size=$(du -h "$zip" | cut -f1)
              echo "  ✅ ZIP: $zip ($size)"
            done
          else
            echo "  ⚠️  No ZIP file found"
          fi

          echo "::endgroup::"

      - name: Set release tag
        id: release_tag
        run: |
          if [ "${{ github.ref_type }}" = "tag" ]; then
            echo "tag=${{ github.ref_name }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "tag=release-latest" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

      - name: Create Release and upload assets
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ steps.release_tag.outputs.tag }}
          target_commitish: ${{ github.sha }}
          files: |
            packages/ui/release/*.dmg
            packages/ui/release/*.zip
          generate_release_notes: true
          draft: false
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

# Build macOS app and attach to GitHub Release.
# Triggers:
#   - Push to main branch
#   - Push of a version tag (e.g. git tag v0.1.0 && git push origin v0.1.0)
# Release URL: https://github.com/OWNER/REPO/releases/latest
#
# For signed + notarized builds, set these repository secrets:
#   APPLE_ID, APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD, APPLE_TEAM_ID
#   CSC_LINK_BASE64 (base64-encoded .p12), CSC_KEY_PASSWORD

name: Release macOS

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
    tags:
      - 'v*'

jobs:
  release-mac:
    runs-on: macos-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Bun
        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: latest

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Verify signing prerequisites
        env:
          CSC_LINK: ${{ secrets.CSC_LINK }}
          CSC_KEY_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.CSC_KEY_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_ID }}
          APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_TEAM_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_TEAM_ID }}
        run: |
          echo "::group::Verifying signing environment variables"

          # Check if all required secrets are set
          if [ -z "$CSC_LINK" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: CSC_LINK is not set"
            echo "ℹ️  CSC_LINK should contain the base64-encoded .p12 certificate"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ CSC_LINK is set (length: ${#CSC_LINK})"
          fi

          if [ -z "$CSC_KEY_PASSWORD" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: CSC_KEY_PASSWORD is not set"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ CSC_KEY_PASSWORD is set"
          fi

          if [ -z "$APPLE_ID" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: APPLE_ID is not set"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ APPLE_ID is set: $APPLE_ID"
          fi

          if [ -z "$APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD is not set"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD is set"
          fi

          if [ -z "$APPLE_TEAM_ID" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: APPLE_TEAM_ID is not set"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ APPLE_TEAM_ID is set: $APPLE_TEAM_ID"
          fi

          echo "::endgroup::"

      - name: Build all packages
        run: |
          # Build contracts, analytics, and server packages first (needed by prepare-server-bundle)
          cd packages/contracts && bun run build
          cd ../analytics && bun run build
          cd ../server && bun run build
          # Build UI (vite + electron typescript)
          cd ../ui
          bunx vite build
          bunx tsc -p tsconfig.electron.json
          bunx tsc -p tsconfig.preload.json

      - name: Package and sign app
        env:
          CSC_LINK_BASE64: ${{ secrets.CSC_LINK_BASE64 }}
          CSC_KEY_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.CSC_KEY_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_ID }}
          APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_TEAM_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_TEAM_ID }}
        run: |
          echo "::group::Packaging and signing"
          cd packages/ui

          # Prepare server bundle (copies dist from server, contracts, analytics)
          echo "📦 Preparing server bundle..."
          node scripts/prepare-server-bundle.mjs

          # Show signing environment (without exposing secrets)
          echo "🔐 Signing configuration:"
          echo "  CSC_LINK: ${CSC_LINK}"
          echo "  CSC_LINK_BASE64: [SET - length: ${#CSC_LINK_BASE64}]"
          echo "  CSC_KEY_PASSWORD: [SET]"
          echo "  APPLE_ID: ${APPLE_ID}"
          echo "  APPLE_TEAM_ID: ${APPLE_TEAM_ID}"
          echo "  APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD: [SET]"

          # Package with signing enabled (electron-builder will use CSC_LINK_BASE64 or CSC_LINK)
          echo "🚀 Building and signing electron app..."
          bun run build:electron:sign

          echo "::endgroup::"

      - name: Verify signed app
        run: |
          echo "::group::Verifying signed application"
          cd packages/ui/release

          # Ensure all file operations are flushed to disk
          sync

          # Find the .app bundle
          app_path=$(find . -name "*.app" -type d | head -n 1)

          if [ -z "$app_path" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: Could not find .app bundle"
            exit 1
          fi

          echo "📱 Found app: $app_path"

          # Debug: Show app bundle structure
          echo "🔍 App bundle structure:"
          ls -la "$app_path/Contents/" || true
          echo ""
          echo "📁 Resources directory:"
          ls -la "$app_path/Contents/Resources/" | head -20 || echo "Resources directory is empty or missing"
          echo ""
          echo "📄 CodeResources file:"
          ls -la "$app_path/Contents/_CodeSignature/" || echo "_CodeSignature directory missing"

          # Verify code signature (without --deep for initial check)
          echo "🔍 Verifying code signature (shallow)..."
          if codesign -vvv --strict "$app_path" 2>&1; then
            echo "✅ Shallow signature verification passed"
          else
            echo "❌ ERROR: Shallow signature verification failed"
            echo "Attempting to show more details..."
            codesign -dvvv "$app_path" 2>&1 || true
            exit 1
          fi

          # Now try deep verification
          echo "🔍 Verifying code signature (deep)..."
          if codesign -vvv --deep --strict "$app_path" 2>&1; then
            echo "✅ Deep signature verification passed"
          else
            echo "⚠️  WARNING: Deep signature verification failed, but shallow passed"
            echo "This may be due to unsigned nested frameworks (common with electron-builder)"
          fi

          # Display signature info
          echo "📋 Signature information:"
          codesign -dvvv "$app_path" 2>&1 | grep -E "(Authority|TeamIdentifier|Identifier|Signature)" || true

          # Check for hardened runtime
          echo "🛡️  Checking hardened runtime..."
          codesign -dvvv "$app_path" 2>&1 | grep -i "runtime" || echo "⚠️  Hardened runtime info not found"

          # Verify Gatekeeper will accept it
          echo "🚪 Verifying Gatekeeper acceptance..."
          spctl -a -vvv -t execute "$app_path" 2>&1 || {
            echo "⚠️  WARNING: Gatekeeper assessment failed (may need notarization to complete)"
          }

          echo "::endgroup::"

      - name: List build artifacts
        run: |
          echo "::group::Build artifacts"
          cd packages/ui/release

          echo "📦 Generated files:"
          ls -lh

          # Check for DMG
          if ls *.dmg 1> /dev/null 2>&1; then
            for dmg in *.dmg; do
              size=$(du -h "$dmg" | cut -f1)
              echo "  ✅ DMG: $dmg ($size)"
            done
          else
            echo "  ❌ No DMG file found"
          fi

          # Check for ZIP
          if ls *.zip 1> /dev/null 2>&1; then
            for zip in *.zip; do
              size=$(du -h "$zip" | cut -f1)
              echo "  ✅ ZIP: $zip ($size)"
            done
          else
            echo "  ⚠️  No ZIP file found"
          fi

          echo "::endgroup::"

      - name: Set release tag
        id: release_tag
        run: |
          if [ "${{ github.ref_type }}" = "tag" ]; then
            echo "tag=${{ github.ref_name }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "tag=release-latest" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

      - name: Create Release and upload assets
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ steps.release_tag.outputs.tag }}
          target_commitish: ${{ github.sha }}
          files: |
            packages/ui/release/*.dmg
            packages/ui/release/*.zip
          generate_release_notes: true
          draft: false
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Build macOS app and attach to GitHub Release.
# Triggers:
#   - Push to main branch
#   - Push of a version tag (e.g. git tag v0.1.0 && git push origin v0.1.0)
# Release URL: https://github.com/OWNER/REPO/releases/latest
#
# For signed + notarized builds, set these repository secrets:
#   APPLE_ID, APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD, APPLE_TEAM_ID
#   CSC_LINK_BASE64 (base64-encoded .p12), CSC_KEY_PASSWORD

name: Release macOS

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
    tags:
      - 'v*'

jobs:
  release-mac:
    runs-on: macos-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Bun
        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: latest

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Verify signing prerequisites
        env:
          CSC_LINK: ${{ secrets.CSC_LINK }}
          CSC_KEY_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.CSC_KEY_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_ID }}
          APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_TEAM_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_TEAM_ID }}
        run: |
          echo "::group::Verifying signing environment variables"

          # Check if all required secrets are set
          if [ -z "$CSC_LINK" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: CSC_LINK is not set"
            echo "ℹ️  CSC_LINK should contain the base64-encoded .p12 certificate"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ CSC_LINK is set (length: ${#CSC_LINK})"
          fi

          if [ -z "$CSC_KEY_PASSWORD" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: CSC_KEY_PASSWORD is not set"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ CSC_KEY_PASSWORD is set"
          fi

          if [ -z "$APPLE_ID" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: APPLE_ID is not set"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ APPLE_ID is set: $APPLE_ID"
          fi

          if [ -z "$APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD is not set"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD is set"
          fi

          if [ -z "$APPLE_TEAM_ID" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: APPLE_TEAM_ID is not set"
            exit 1
          else
            echo "✅ APPLE_TEAM_ID is set: $APPLE_TEAM_ID"
          fi

          echo "::endgroup::"

      - name: Build all packages
        run: |
          # Build contracts, analytics, and server packages first (needed by prepare-server-bundle)
          cd packages/contracts && bun run build
          cd ../analytics && bun run build
          cd ../server && bun run build
          # Build UI (vite + electron typescript)
          cd ../ui
          bunx vite build
          bunx tsc -p tsconfig.electron.json
          bunx tsc -p tsconfig.preload.json

      - name: Package and sign app
        env:
          CSC_LINK: ${{ secrets.CSC_LINK }}
          CSC_KEY_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.CSC_KEY_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_ID }}
          APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD }}
          APPLE_TEAM_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_TEAM_ID }}
        run: |
          echo "::group::Packaging and signing"
          cd packages/ui

          # Prepare server bundle (copies dist from server, contracts, analytics)
          echo "📦 Preparing server bundle..."
          node scripts/prepare-server-bundle.mjs

          # Show signing environment (without exposing secrets)
          echo "🔐 Signing configuration:"
          echo "  CSC_LINK: [SET - length: ${#CSC_LINK}]"
          echo "  CSC_KEY_PASSWORD: [SET]"
          echo "  APPLE_ID: ${APPLE_ID}"
          echo "  APPLE_TEAM_ID: ${APPLE_TEAM_ID}"
          echo "  APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD: [SET]"

          # Package with signing enabled (electron-builder will use CSC_LINK with base64 data)
          echo "🚀 Building and signing electron app..."
          bun run build:electron:sign

          echo "::endgroup::"

      - name: Verify signed app
        run: |
          echo "::group::Verifying signed application"
          cd packages/ui/release

          # Ensure all file operations are flushed to disk
          sync

          # Find the .app bundle
          app_path=$(find . -name "*.app" -type d | head -n 1)

          if [ -z "$app_path" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: Could not find .app bundle"
            exit 1
          fi

          echo "📱 Found app: $app_path"

          # Debug: Show app bundle structure
          echo "🔍 App bundle structure:"
          ls -la "$app_path/Contents/" || true
          echo ""
          echo "📁 Resources directory:"
          ls -la "$app_path/Contents/Resources/" | head -20 || echo "Resources directory is empty or missing"
          echo ""
          echo "📄 CodeResources file:"
          ls -la "$app_path/Contents/_CodeSignature/" || echo "_CodeSignature directory missing"

          # Verify code signature (without --deep for initial check)
          echo "🔍 Verifying code signature (shallow)..."
          if codesign -vvv --strict "$app_path" 2>&1; then
            echo "✅ Shallow signature verification passed"
          else
            echo "❌ ERROR: Shallow signature verification failed"
            echo "Attempting to show more details..."
            codesign -dvvv "$app_path" 2>&1 || true
            exit 1
          fi

          # Now try deep verification
          echo "🔍 Verifying code signature (deep)..."
          if codesign -vvv --deep --strict "$app_path" 2>&1; then
            echo "✅ Deep signature verification passed"
          else
            echo "⚠️  WARNING: Deep signature verification failed, but shallow passed"
            echo "This may be due to unsigned nested frameworks (common with electron-builder)"
          fi

          # Display signature info
          echo "📋 Signature information:"
          codesign -dvvv "$app_path" 2>&1 | grep -E "(Authority|TeamIdentifier|Identifier|Signature)" || true

          # Check for hardened runtime
          echo "🛡️  Checking hardened runtime..."
          codesign -dvvv "$app_path" 2>&1 | grep -i "runtime" || echo "⚠️  Hardened runtime info not found"

          # Verify Gatekeeper will accept it
          echo "🚪 Verifying Gatekeeper acceptance..."
          spctl -a -vvv -t execute "$app_path" 2>&1 || {
            echo "⚠️  WARNING: Gatekeeper assessment failed (may need notarization to complete)"
          }

          echo "::endgroup::"

      - name: List build artifacts
        run: |
          echo "::group::Build artifacts"
          cd packages/ui/release

          echo "📦 Generated files:"
          ls -lh

          # Check for DMG
          if ls *.dmg 1> /dev/null 2>&1; then
            for dmg in *.dmg; do
              size=$(du -h "$dmg" | cut -f1)
              echo "  ✅ DMG: $dmg ($size)"
            done
          else
            echo "  ❌ No DMG file found"
          fi

          # Check for ZIP
          if ls *.zip 1> /dev/null 2>&1; then
            for zip in *.zip; do
              size=$(du -h "$zip" | cut -f1)
              echo "  ✅ ZIP: $zip ($size)"
            done
          else
            echo "  ⚠️  No ZIP file found"
          fi

          echo "::endgroup::"

      - name: Set release tag
        id: release_tag
        run: |
          if [ "${{ github.ref_type }}" = "tag" ]; then
            echo "tag=${{ github.ref_name }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "tag=release-latest" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

      - name: Create Release and upload assets
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ steps.release_tag.outputs.tag }}
          target_commitish: ${{ github.sha }}
          files: |
            packages/ui/release/*.dmg
            packages/ui/release/*.zip
          generate_release_notes: true
          draft: false
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

fix(ci): use CSC_LINK instead of CSC_LINK_BASE64 for electron-builder signing

electron-builder expects CSC_LINK to contain the base64-encoded certificate
data directly, not a separate CSC_LINK_BASE64 variable. This removes the
manual certificate decoding step and lets electron-builder handle it.

Changes:
- Rename CSC_LINK_BASE64 to CSC_LINK throughout workflow
- Remove "Prepare code signing certificate" step (no longer needed)
- Update comments to reflect correct secret name

NOTE: Repository secret CSC_LINK_BASE64 must be renamed to CSC_LINK

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
